Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 301 [Babylonian Almanac][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
o i 1o i 1

[...] U₄ 1-KAM₂ ka-liš ma-gir

(o i 1) [...] the 1st day: it is totally favourable.

o i 22

[...] U₄.SA₉-AM₃ ŠE.GA

(o i 2) [...] (at) midday: it is favourable.

o i 33

[...]-⸢KAM₂⸣ U₄.SA₉-AM₃ ŠE.GA

(o i 3) [...] (at) midday: it is favourable.

o i 44

[...]-KAM₂ A.ŠA₃.GA AN.DIB.BA

(o i 4) [...] he will go along the field.

o i 55

[...]-KAM₂ ŠA₃.ḪUL₂.LA LA₂

(o i 5) [...] happiness ....

o i 66

[...]-KAM₂ in E₂ ma-gir

(o i 6) [...] in the house, it is favourable.

o i 77

[...] ⸢6⸣-KAM₂ U₃.MA SA₂.SA₂

(o i 7) [...] 6th day: he will achieve triumph.

o i 88

[...] 10?-KAM₂ gišTUKUL SAG!(KA)KI.DAB.BA

(o i 8) [...] 10th day: stroke of sankidabbû-illness.

o i 99

[...] 12-KAM₂ U₄.SA₉-AM₃ ŠE.GA

(o i 9) [...] (at) midday: it is favourable.

o i 1010

[*] ⸢ina⸣ itiGU₄ U₄ 2-KAM₂ ŠE.GA

(o i 10) In Ayyaru (Month II), the 2nd day is favourable.

o i 1111

[*] ⸢U₄⸣ 5-KAM DAM TUKUši LIBIRra

(o i 11) On the 5th day: he should acquire a wife; (or) he will live long.

o i 1212

[*] ⸢U₄⸣ 8-KAM₂ IDIM ŠE.GA

(o i 12) On the 8th day: (as for) the nobleman, it is favourable.

o i 1313

[*] ⸢U₄⸣ 10-KAM₂ in di-nim ŠE.GA

(o i 13) On the 10th day: during a trial, it is favourable.

o i 1414

[*] ⸢U₄⸣ 12-KAM₂ in SILA ŠE.GA

(o i 14) On the 12th day: in the street, it is favourable.

o i 1515

[*] ⸢U₄⸣ 15-KAM₂ NUMUN ŠU.DU₇

(o i 15) On the 15th day: perfect seed.

o i 1616

[*] ⸢U₄⸣ 16-KAM₂ ŠA₃.ḪUL₂.LA

(o i 16) On the 16th day: happiness.

o i 1717

[*] ⸢U₄⸣ 18-KAM₂ za-ku-ut pu-šu-ur

(o i 17) On the 18th day: unpack the winnowed grain.

o i 1818

[...] 20?-KAM₂ MUŠ ḪE₂.EN.⸢GAZ⸣ SAG.KAL DU

(o i 18) [...] 20th day: may he kill a snake; (or) he will take first place.

o i 1919

[...]-⸢KAM₂⸣ INIMnim KUR DU₃eš AŠ₂

(o i 19) [...] affairs will be successful; accomplishing a desire.

o i 2020

[...]-KAM₂ ŠA₃.ḪUL₂.LA

(o i 20) [...] happiness.

o i 2121

[...]-KAM₂ bu-su-ra-tu₄

(o i 21) [...] news.

o i 2222

[...] ⸢26?⸣-KAM₂ ka-liš ma-gir

(o i 22) [...] the 26th day is totally favourable.

o i 2323

[...] 29v?-KAM₂ MUŠ ḪE₂.EN.GAZ MIN<(ka-liš)> ma-gir

(o i 23) [...] (on) the 29th: may he kill a snake; it is totally favourable.

o i 2424

[...]-KAM₂ GISKIM SA₆

(o i 24) [...] the sign is favourable.

o i 2525

[...] x ⸢U₄⸣ 3-KAM₂ ŠE.GA

(o i 25) [...] the 3rd day is favourable.

o i 2626

[...] x U₃.MA GABA.RI-A

(o i 26) [...] ... the rival's triumph.

o i 2727

[...] ⸢ŠA₃⸣.ḪUL₂.LA

(o i 27) [...] happiness.

o i 2828

[...] ⸢ka⸣-liš ma-gir

(o i 28) [...] it is totally favourable.

o i 2929

[...] E₂ in E₂ ma-gir

(o i 29) [...] of the house, in the house, it is favourable.

o i 3030

[...] in SILA ma-gir

(o i 30) [...] in the street, it is favourable.

o i 3131

[...] ⸢ŠE.GA⸣

(o i 31) [...] it is favourable.

x [...]

U₄ 7-⸢KAM₂⸣ [...]

(o ii 3) On the 7th day [...].

o ii 44

U₄ 11-KAM₂ ka-liš ma-⸢gir⸣

(o ii 4) The 11th day is totally favourable.

o ii 55

U₄ 12-KAM₂ ša₂-se-e LUGAL

(o ii 5) On the 12th day: summoning of the king.

o ii 66

[U₄] 13-KAM₂ DINGIR TUKU ŠE.GA

(o ii 6) On the 13th day: he will acquire a (protective) god; it is favourable.

o ii 77

U₄ 17-KAM₂ ina E₂ ma-gir SAG.ARAD ŠE

(o ii 7) On the 17th day: in the house, it is favourable; (as for) the slave, it is favourable.

o ii 88

KIMIN<(U₄)> 17-KAM₂ za-kar MU TUKUši

(o ii 8) On ditto (the 17th day): he will acquire a good reputation.

o ii 99

U₄ 19v-KAM₂ ŠE.GA LUGAL-LA.KA

(o ii 9) On the 19th day: favourable for the king.

o ii 1010

U₄ 21-KAM₂ ŠE.GA LUGAL-LA.KA

(o ii 10) On the 21st day: favourable for the king.

o ii 1111

U₄ ⸢22?⸣-KAM₂ GAŠAN ŠE.GA ŠA₃.ḪUL₂.LA

(o ii 11) On the 22nd day: the goddess is favourable; happiness.

o ii 1212

U₄ 26-KAM₂ SAG.ARAD ŠE.GA

(o ii 12) On the 26th day: as for the slave, it is favourable.

o ii 1313

U₄ 27-KAM₂ in di-nim ma-gir

(o ii 13) On the 27th day: during a trial, it is favourable.

o ii 1414

U₄ 28-KAM₂ ka-liš ma-gir

(o ii 14) The 28th day is totally favourable.

o ii 1515

U₄ 29v-KAM₂ ka-liš ma-gir

(o ii 15) The 29th day is totally favourable.

o ii 1616

* ina itiNE U₄ 1-KAM₂ GAŠAN <<x>> ŠE.GA

(o ii 16) In Abu (Month V), on the 1st day: the goddess is favourable.

o ii 1717

U₄ 2-KAM₂ ki-mil-ti DINGIR ŠE.GA

(o ii 17) On the 2nd day: divine wrath; (or) it is favourable.

o ii 1818

U₄ 6-KAM₂ U₄.SA₉-⸢AM₃⸣ ŠE.GA

(o ii 18) On the 6th day, (at) midday: it is favourable.

o ii 1919

U₄ 10-KAM₂ KURUM₆-su ana dGU.LA  ; GARun ŠE.GA

(o ii 19) On the 10th day: may he set in place his offering for Gula; it is favourable.

o ii 2020

U₄ 12-KAM NU.ŠE.GA

(o ii 20) The 12th day is unfavourable.

o ii 2121

U₄ 15-KAM₂ AB.ŠE.GA

(o ii 21) The 15th day is favourable.

o ii 2222

U₄ 16-KAM₂ AB.ŠE.GA

(o ii 22) The 16th day is favourable.

o ii 2323

* ina itiKIN U₄ 1-KAM₂ AB.ŠE.GA

(o ii 23) In Ululu (Month VI), the 1st day is favourable.

o ii 2424

U₄ 2-KAM₂ AB.ŠE.GA

(o ii 24) The 2nd day is favourable.

o ii 2525

U₄ 3-KAM₂ AB.ŠE.GA

(o ii 25) The 3rd day is favourable.

o ii 2626

U₄ 5-KAM₂ AB.ŠE.GA

(o ii 26) The 5th day is favourable.

(r ii 8') On the 27th day: it is favourable.

U₄ 30-KAM₂ ka-liš ma-⸢gir⸣

(r ii 9') The 30st day is totally favourable.

(catchline)
r ii 10'10'

* UŠ₈! E₂! BAD-⸢ma⸣ SIG₄! ⸢ŠUB⸣di

(r ii 10') (If) he opens (a hole for) the house's foundations and lays down the (first) mudbrick.

(colophon)
r ii 11'11'

ša₃-ṭir₂ dMUATI—KARir-an-ni

(r ii 11') Writer: Nabu-eṭiranni.

r ii 12'12'

ina itiGAN U₄ 26-KAM₂

(r ii 12') In Kislimu (Month IX), on the 26th day, eponym: Nergal-šarru-uṣur, chief cupbearer.

r ii 13'13'

lim-mu

r ii 14'14'

mdNERGALₓ(U.GUR)—MAN—PAB

r ii 15'15'

lu₂GAL.KAŠ.LUL

r ii 16'16'

a-na IGI.DU₈.A

(r ii 16') For the viewing of Bel-ah-iddin, the šangû-priest.

r ii 17'17'

mdEN—PAP—AŠ

r ii 18'18'

lu₂E₂.BAR

Column iii
(rest of column missing)
r iii 1'r 1'

[...]-⸢d⸣IGI.DU

(r iii 1') [...]-Ninurta;

r iii 2'2'

[...] ⸢IGI⸣.DU₈.A

(r iii 2') [...] the viewing of [...] ...-uṣur, the novice;

r iii 3'3'

[...] x-x-x-ŠEŠ

r iii 4'4'

a-⸢ga-aš⸣-gu-u₂

r iii 5'5'

[a]-⸢na⸣ IGI.DU₈.A

(r iii 5') for the viewing of Rimut-ilani, the junior physician;

r iii 6'6'

⸢m⸣ri-mut—DINGIR-MEŠni

r iii 7'7'

lu₂A.ZU ṣe-eḫ-ri

r iii 8'8'

a-na IGI.DU₈.A mNUMUN—GUB

(r iii 8') for the viewing of Zer-ukin, the junior scribal apprentice:

r iii 9'9'

lu₂šam-lu₃-u₂

r iii 10'10'

⸢ṣe⸣-eḫ-ri

r iii 11'11'

[a]-⸢na⸣ IGI.DU₈-šu₂-nu

(r iii 11') for their viewing, it has been quickly excerpted.

r iii 12'12'

[ḫa]-⸢an⸣-ṭiš ZIiḫ

1The text bears a scribal mistake, similar to the one in CTN 4, 56 obv iii 19'': initial i- has been forgotten. Unlike CTN 4, this text bears IM and not IH.
